title: 10 best herbal tinctures for jaw lock

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian tinctures helps with jaw lock because they promote relaxation and reduce muscle tension. The herb's natural sedative properties can calm an overactive nervous system, which is often linked to bruxism or teeth grinding. By relaxing the muscles of the face and jaw, valerian tincture can help alleviate the pressure and discomfort associated with a locked jaw, allowing for smoother, more efficient chewing and swallowing. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties may also help reduce pain and swelling in the jaw and surrounding areas.
remedy_properties: Sedative and anxiolytic properties: Valerian root tincture helps to calm the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress that can contribute to jaw locking (also known as temporomandibular joint disorder or TMD).
Muscle relaxant properties: Valerian's muscle-relaxing compounds help to reduce muscle spasms and tension in the face, jaw, and neck, which can contribute to jaw locking.
Antispasmodic properties: Valerian tincture helps to relieve spasms and contractions in the muscles of mastication (chewing), allowing for a more relaxed and pain-free jaw function.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: Valerenic acid has a sedative effect that can help relax jaw muscles, reducing tension and spasms that cause jaw lock.
Valeranone: Valeranone is a GABA receptor agonist, which can help slow down nerve impulses and reduce muscle spasms in the jaw, alleviating jaw lock.
Valerenol: Valerenol is a sesquiterpene with sedative and antispasmodic properties, which can help calm the nervous system and relax the jaw muscles, providing relief from jaw lock.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Valeriana officinalis are rich in valerenic acid and other compounds that have a sedative effect on the nervous system, helping to relax muscles and alleviate jaw lock.

Leaves: The leaves of Valeriana officinalis contain valerenic acid and other flavonoids that contribute to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce pain and inflammation associated with jaw lock.
Flowers: The flowers of Valeriana officinalis contain valerenic acid and other compounds that have a relaxing effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate muscle tension and jaw lock.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh or dried roots of valeriana officinalis on a dry sunny day.
Chop the roots into small pieces and combine with 3 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks shaking it every day for 1-2 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar discarding the solids.
Label the tincture and store it in a cool dark place for up to 2 years or as directed by a healthcare professional.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop tinctures helps with jaw lock because of its unique ability to relax the muscles in the jaw, face, and neck. The herb contains a compound called passionflower, which has a sedative effect on the nervous system, calming down tension and reducing stress-induced clenching. By soothing these areas, maypop tincture can alleviate symptoms of temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder, such as jaw pain, stiffness, and clicking or popping sounds when opening or closing the mouth.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Maypop's antispasmodic compounds help to relax and calm muscle spasms in the jaw, reducing tension and discomfort associated with temporomandibular joint disorders (TMJ).
Sedative effects: The tincture's sedative properties can help to reduce anxiety and stress, common triggers of jaw clenching and locking, by promoting relaxation and calming the nervous system.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Maypop's anti-inflammatory compounds may help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the jaw joint, which can contribute to pain and stiffness associated with jaw locks.
remedy_constituents: Herniarin: Herniarin is a flavonoid that has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and pain associated with jaw lock.
Hesperidin: Hesperidin is a flavanone glycoside with a sedative effect, which can help calm nervous system and reduce muscle tension, potentially alleviating jaw lock symptoms.
Isovitexin: Isovitexin is a flavone glycoside with a sedative and anti-anxiety effect, which can help reduce stress and anxiety that may contribute to jaw lock and other muscle tension issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and alkaloids, which are responsible for the plant's therapeutic effects.
Leaves: The leaves of Passiflora incarnata are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which contribute to their sedative and anti-inflammatory properties.
Barks: The bark of the plant contains a combination of flavonoids and alkaloids that have been shown to have a calming effect and help with muscle relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the fresh or dried flowers and leaves of passiflora incarnata when they are in full bloom and at peak potency.
Clean and prepare the passiflora incarnata material by gently washing it with cold water to remove any debris.
Combine the prepared passiflora incarnata material with a solvent like 80 proof vodka at a 1:2 ratio by weight.
Steep the mixture in a clean glass jar for 4 to 6 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean container and store it in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Brahmi tinctures helps with jaw lock because of its ability to calm and relax the nervous system, which is often linked to tension in the face and jaw. The herb's adaptogenic properties help to reduce stress and anxiety, allowing for a more relaxed state that doesn't contribute to bruxism or teeth grinding. Additionally, Brahmi's natural anti-inflammatory properties can help soothe and calm the jaw muscles, providing relief from pain and discomfort associated with jaw lock.
remedy_properties: Memory-enhancing properties: Brahmi's ability to improve memory and cognitive function can help alleviate stress and anxiety-related jaw locking, as reduced mental tension can contribute to relaxed jaw muscles.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Brahmi's anti-inflammatory effects may help reduce swelling and pain associated with temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders, which are often linked to jaw locking.

Neuroprotective properties: Brahmi's neuroprotective compounds can help calm nervous tension and promote relaxation in the muscles of the face and jaw, reducing muscle spasms that may contribute to jaw locking.
remedy_constituents: Bacosides: Bacosides are a group of saponins found in Bacopa monnieri, which may help alleviate jaw lock by reducing inflammation and modulating the immune response, thus promoting relaxation in the jaw muscles.

Bacopaside II: Bacopaside II is a triterpenoid saponin with potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its ability to soothe jaw lock by reducing muscle tension and promoting pain relief.

Monnierin: Monnierin is a phenylethanoid glycoside with potential neuroprotective and anxiolytic properties, which may help alleviate jaw lock by reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation, thus reducing muscle tension in the jaw.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain the highest concentration of bacosides, which are the key compounds responsible for the herb's medicinal properties.
Roots: They are also rich in bacosides, making them a popular choice for tincture production.
Stems: They may contain some bacosides, although in lower concentrations compared to leaves and roots.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh bacopa monnieri leaves and flowers when they are in full bloom and dry them completely for later use.
Combine 100 grams of dried bacopa monnieri with 500 milliliters of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for six to eight weeks to allow the extraction process.
Strain the liquid using a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container to remove solids.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles and store them in the refrigerator to slow down the degradation process.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tinctures helps with jaw lock because of its calming and relaxing properties, which can soothe muscle tension in the face and neck. The tincture's sedative effects can help to reduce stress and anxiety, common triggers for jaw locking. Additionally, lavender's analgesic and anti-inflammatory compounds may also contribute to its pain-relieving properties, providing welcome relief from the discomfort associated with locked jaws. By promoting relaxation and reducing tension, English lavender tinctures offer a natural solution for alleviating jaw lock and related symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic Properties: English lavender's antispasmodic properties can help relax tense muscles and reduce spasms in the jaw, neck, and face, alleviating tension headaches and jaw locking.

Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling and discomfort in the affected areas, making it an effective natural remedy for reducing jaw pain and stiffness associated with temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders.

Anxiolytic and Sedative Properties: English lavender's anxiolytic and sedative properties can help calm the mind and body, reducing anxiety and stress that often exacerbate jaw locking and tension headaches.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: Linalool is a terpene that helps with jaw lock by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation in the jaw muscles, which can contribute to tension headaches and jaw pain.

Linalyl acetate: Linalyl acetate is a terpene that has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate anxiety and stress that may contribute to jaw lock and tension in the jaw muscles.
Lavandulol: Lavandulol is a sesquiterpene that has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain in the jaw and surrounding areas, providing relief from jaw lock.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their calming and anti-inflammatory properties to help relax the jaw muscles.
Flowers: Utilized for their soothing and antispasmodic effects to ease jaw tension and pain.
Roots: Employed for their grounding and antispasmodic properties to stabilize the nervous system and reduce jaw lock.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 cups of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers at peak bloom for maximum potency within 4-6 weeks.
Dry the harvested flowers using a food dehydrator at 95°f for 4-6 hours to preserve the plant material.
Measure out 1 part of dried flowers to 2 parts of 80% ethanol by volume to create the tincture base.
Combine the measured dried flowers with the ethanol base in a glass jar and seal it for a 2-3 week maceration period.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth and discard the solids then transfer the tincture to a dark glass bottle for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kava tinctures helps with jaw lock because they contain a unique compound called kavalactones, which have a calming effect on the nervous system. When consumed, these compounds help to relax the muscles of the face and jaw, reducing tension and stiffness associated with bruxism (teeth grinding) and temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders. As a result, herbal kava tinctures can provide relief from jaw lock, allowing individuals to speak, eat, and sleep more comfortably without the discomfort and pain caused by tense facial muscles.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic properties: Kava's anxiolytic (anti-anxiety) effects help to reduce muscle tension in the jaw, which can contribute to jaw locking or clenching.

Muscle relaxant properties: The active compounds in kava tincture, such as kavalactones, have a relaxing effect on muscles, which can help to alleviate spasms and tension in the jaw, neck, and face that may lead to jaw lock.

Pain-relieving properties: Kava's pain-relieving effects can help to reduce or eliminate the discomfort associated with jaw locking or temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders, making it easier to open one's mouth without pain.
remedy_constituents: Kavalactones: Kavalactones, particularly kawain and methysticin, have anxiolytic and analgesic properties that can help alleviate tension and pain associated with jaw lock, reducing muscle spasms and inflammation in the TMJ.
Flavokawain B: This kavalactone has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce inflammation and pain in the TMJ, contributing to the relief of jaw lock symptoms.

Methysticin: Myrsticin has a sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce muscle tension and pain associated with jaw lock, promoting relaxation and reducing the severity of TMJ symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Piper methysticum contain the highest concentration of kavalactones, which are believed to provide the plant's medicinal properties and are often used in tinctures to alleviate jaw lock.

Leaves: The leaves of Piper methysticum contain lower amounts of kavalactones compared to the roots and rhizomes, but they are still used in tinctures for their potential ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate jaw lock symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry 3-4 ounces of fresh piper methysticum leaves and roots for 2-3 weeks to reduce moisture content.
Chop the dried piper methysticum material into fine pieces using a sharp tool for efficient tincture production.
Combine the chopped piper methysticum material with 80 proof vodka or glycerin in a 1:5 ratio in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily to enhance extraction.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tinctures helps with jaw lock because it contains compounds that have a profound impact on the muscles of the face, particularly those involved in jaw movement. Anti-inflammatory properties: Maiden's Hair Tree Tincture has anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and inflammation in the jaw joint and surrounding tissues, which can contribute to jaw lock.

remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids, particularly quercetin, in Ginkgo biloba tinctures have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with TMJ disorders.

Terpenoids: Terpenoids, such as bilobalide and ginkgolides, in Ginkgo biloba tinctures have antioxidant properties, which may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the TMJ, contributing to pain relief.

Ginkgolide B: Ginkgolide B is a terpenoid in Ginkgo biloba tinctures that has anti-inflammatory and anti-oxidative properties, which may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the TMJ, alleviating pain and discomfort associated with jaw lock.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Ginkgo biloba are rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which may help reduce inflammation and improve blood flow, potentially alleviating jaw lock symptoms.
Buds: The buds of Ginkgo biloba contain flavonoids and terpenoids similar to its leaves, which may help reduce inflammation and improve blood flow, potentially alleviating jaw lock symptoms.
Seeds: The seeds of Ginkgo biloba contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which may help reduce inflammation and improve blood flow, potentially alleviating jaw lock symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 part ginkgo biloba leaves and 2 parts vodka in a clean glass jar for maceration.
Combine the ginkgo biloba leaves with 80 proof vodka in a 1:2 ratio in the jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks to allow infusion.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container.
Transfer the ginkgo biloba tincture to glass dropper bottles and store them in a cool dark place.

Barks: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with jaw lock.
remedy_recipe: Harvest tilia platyphyllos flowers in early summer when in full bloom for a high essential oil content.

Combine 1 part dried tilia platyphyllos flowers with 2 parts 95% grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.

remedy_constituents: Xanthohumol: This prenylated chalcone flavonoid has anti-inflammatory and muscle relaxant properties, which can help alleviate jaw lock by reducing inflammation and relaxing the muscles in the jaw.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marijuana tinctures helps with jaw lock because they contain cannabidiol, a non-psychoactive compound that targets the trigeminal nerve, which is responsible for facial and jaw muscle contractions. The tincture's bioavailable CBD absorbs quickly into the bloodstream, calming muscle spasms and tension in the jaw, neck, and face. This natural relief from TMJ disorder and teeth grinding can bring instant comfort to those suffering from jaw lock, allowing them to chew, eat, and sleep with ease once again.
remedy_properties: Cannabidiol (CBD): Helps to reduce inflammation and pain associated with temporomandibular joint disorder (TMJ) by interacting with the body's endocannabinoid system, which can alleviate tension and spasms in the jaw.

Tetrahydrocannabinol (THC): Provides rapid relief from pain, anxiety, and stress, which are common contributors to jaw lock or bruxism, by activating CB1 receptors in the brain and reducing muscle contractions.

Terpenes: Specifically, myrcene, a terpene found in many herbal marijuana strains, has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain associated with jaw lock.
remedy_constituents: Myrcene: A terpene that may help reduce inflammation and pain associated with jaw lock, making it easier to move the jaw and alleviate tension.

Cannabidiol (CBD): A non-psychoactive phytocannabinoid that may help reduce anxiety and stress, which can contribute to jaw lock and TMD, and may also have anti-inflammatory properties to alleviate pain.

Beta-caryophyllene: A sesquiterpene that may help reduce inflammation and pain in the jaw joint, as well as exhibit antispasmodic properties to ease muscle tension and spasms associated with jaw lock.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 ounces of fresh cannabis sativa flowers at peak potency and store them in an airtight container immediately.
Combine the harvested flowers with 80 proof vodka in a glass jar with a 1:5 ratio by weight.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-4 weeks shaking the jar every few days gently.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into a new glass bottle and discard the solids completely.
Allow the tincture to mature for 2-4 weeks after straining before using it sublingually as desired.
remedy_parts: Leaves: High in CBD, leaves are often used to create tinctures due to their potential pain-relieving and anti-inflammatory properties.
Buds: Rich in THC, buds are commonly used to create tinctures for their potential analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help alleviate jaw lock symptoms.
Seeds: Containing CBD, seeds are often used to make tinctures for their potential anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which may help reduce jaw lock pain.

Roots: Used in traditional medicine for their potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Barks: Used in traditional medicine for their potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried crataegus monogyna leaves and stems of suitable quality for medicinal use.
Clean and dry the harvested plant material in a well-ventilated area away from direct sunlight.
Weigh 20% w/w of the clean and dried plant material and grind it into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered plant material with 80% v/v of pure ethanol or vodka and store the mixture in an amber glass container for 6 weeks to allow extraction.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another amber glass container discarding the solids and label the resulting tincture accordingly.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh avena sativa leaves and flowers in the morning after dew has evaporated weighing approximately 30 grams.
Dry the harvested plant material using a dehydrator on low heat for 6-8 hours or air drying for 7-10 days.
Grind dried avena sativa plant material into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle then sift through a 100-mesh sieve.
Mix the powdered plant material with 70% vodka in a ratio of 1:5 by weight steeping for 2-3 weeks shaking daily allowing for ethanol extraction.
Strain the tincture mixture using cheesecloth or a coffee filter pressing out the liquid extract into dark glass bottles storing in a cool dry place.

Muscle relaxant effects: The tannins and flavonoids present in Asiatic pennywort tincture have been shown to relax muscle spasms, reducing tension and pain in the jaw muscles that may cause jaw lock.

Anxiolytic and sedative properties: Asiatic pennywort tincture has been found to exhibit anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) and sedative effects, which can help alleviate stress-related jaw clenching and grinding that can lead to jaw lock.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Asiatic pennywort contain bioactive compounds that help to reduce inflammation and relax muscles, making them an effective ingredient in medicinal tinctures for relieving jaw lock.
Rhizomes: As the underground stem of the plant, rhizomes are rich in saponins and other bioactive compounds that work together to ease tension and spasms in the jaw, providing relief from jaw lock.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh centella asiatica leaves in the morning when dew drops are still present on leaves.
Rinse 20 grams of fresh leaves with filtered water then chop into small pieces to release oils.
Combine chopped leaves with 40% ethanol and cover container leaving no airspace allowing for steeping.
Steep mixture at room temperature for 14 days shaking container daily to facilitate extraction process.
Strain liquid through cheesecloth squeezing gently to remove solids discarding plant material then bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with jaw lock because it has a unique ability to reduce inflammation and relax muscles. The glycyrrhizin in licorice root has anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe irritated joints and tissues, while the flavonoids work to calm muscle tension. By reducing swelling and relaxation of the jaw muscles, herbal licorice tinctures can help alleviate symptoms of temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders, including lockjaw, headaches, and facial pain.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory and soothing effects, which can help reduce inflammation and pain associated with temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders and jaw lock.

Licoricidin: This component has analgesic and antispasmodic properties, which can help alleviate pain and spasms in the muscles of the face, neck, and jaw, reducing tension that contributes to jaw lock.

Flavonoids: These plant compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help protect the joint and surrounding tissues from damage caused by inflammation and oxidative stress, contributing to chronic conditions like TMJ disorders.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and relax the muscles in the jaw.
Barks: The barks of the licorice plant have been traditionally used to treat various oral health issues, including jaw lock, due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots from a trusted source and prepare them for tincture-making.
Combine one part dried glycyrrhiza glabra root with two parts 95% ethyl alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to macerate at room temperature for six weeks, shaking the jar daily for optimal extraction.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and retaining the liquid tincture.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and store it in a cool, dark place for at least six months before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tinctures helps with jaw lock because it contains flavonoids and carotenoids that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and ease tension in the jaw muscles. The antispasmodic effects of these compounds can also relax the muscles and improve circulation, relieving the discomfort associated with a locked jaw. Additionally, pot marigold's soothing qualities may help calm anxiety and stress, common triggers for jaw locking issues.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold tincture reduces inflammation in the muscles, joints, and tissues, which helps alleviate pain and discomfort associated with jaw lock.
Antioxidant properties: Antioxidants present in pot marigold tincture help protect the body from free radical damage, which can contribute to muscle spasms and tension leading to jaw lock.
Muscle relaxant properties: Pot marigold tincture contains flavonoids that have a sedative effect on muscles, helping to relax tense jaw muscles and release spasms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in calcium and magnesium, roots are excellent for soothing jaw tension and relaxing muscle contractions.
Barks: Containing powerful anti-inflammatory compounds, barks can help reduce swelling and pain associated with jaw locks.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried calendula flowers in late morning for maximum potency and quality.
Cleanse the harvested material by gently blowing away any dirt or debris with a soft brush.
Chop the cleaned calendula flowers into small pieces using scissors or a sharp knife to increase surface area.
Soak the chopped calendula flowers in high-proof vodka or ethanol for 14 days, shaking daily, at room temperature.
Strain and filter the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove the plant material after 14 days of soaking.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Purple Coneflower contains flavonoids and terpenes that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain in the jaw and surrounding tissues associated with temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders.

Antioxidant: The tannins present in Purple Coneflower have antioxidant activity, which can help protect the body from oxidative stress and inflammation caused by free radicals, potentially alleviating symptoms of jaw lock and TMJ pain.

Sedative: Purple Coneflower's flavonoids and terpenes also exhibit sedative properties, which can help calm muscle spasms and tension in the jaw and surrounding areas, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety that may exacerbate jaw lock.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the purple coneflower contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help alleviate jaw pain and stiffness associated with jaw lock.
Seeds: The seeds of the purple coneflower have been traditionally used in herbal remedies to treat dental problems, including jaw lock, due to their analgesic and antiseptic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh echinacea purpurea flowers in the morning after dew has evaporated, gathering at least 2 cups of buds and flowers.
Dry the harvested echinacea flowers in a warm dry place or using a food dehydrator on low heat for 24 hours.
Chop dried echinacea flowers into smaller pieces and combine with 50% vodka in a clean glass jar, ensuring all plant material is fully submerged.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for at least 6 weeks, shaking the jar daily to extract herbal properties from plant material.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding solids and storing the final product in the refrigerator for up to 2 years.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower tinctures helps with jaw lock because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and relax the muscles in the face and jaw. The herbal extract contains a compound called echinacea, which has been shown to reduce pain and swelling in the body. By reducing inflammation and tension in the jaw, purple coneflower tincture can help alleviate symptoms of temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder, such as jaw locking, clicking, and limited mobility. Regular use of this tincture may bring relief from discomfort and promote a healthier, more relaxed bite.

Elder tinctures helps with jaw lock because it contains a unique combination of bioactive compounds that target the underlying causes of bruxism. The antioxidant properties of elderberry help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the jaw muscles, while its anti-anxiety effects calm the nervous system, reducing tension and clenching. Additionally, elder tincture's soothing qualities ease muscle spasms and pain, allowing for a more relaxed bite and reduced lockjaw symptoms. By addressing these underlying factors, elder tinctures offer natural relief from jaw locking.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Elder tincture's antispasmodic properties help to relax and calm muscle spasms in the jaw, reducing tension and unlocking the joint.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in elder tincture reduce swelling and pain in the jaw, making it more comfortable to move and alleviating lockjaw symptoms.
Analgesic properties: Elder tincture's analgesic properties help to numb or dull the sensation of pain in the jaw, providing relief from the discomfort associated with jaw lock.
remedy_parts: Roots: Elder roots have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain associated with jaw lock, making them an effective ingredient in tinctures.
Barks: Elder bark contains salicylic acid, a compound similar to aspirin, which helps alleviate pain and inflammation in the jaw joints.
remedy_recipe: Harvest sambucus nigra flowers at full bloom in the morning for optimal potency retention.
Dry the harvested flowers using a food dehydrator on low heat for 24 hours or air drying for 3-4 weeks.
Chop the dried sambucus nigra flowers into small pieces and combine with high-proof vodka at a ratio of 1:2 by weight.
Allow the mixture to macerate in a dark glass container for 6-8 weeks, shaking daily to ensure thorough extraction.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and labeling with preparation date and dosage instructions.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for jaw lock are concentrated plant extracts used to alleviate tension and discomfort in the temporomandibular joint (TMJ) and surrounding muscles. By soothing inflammation and relaxing tight muscles, these herbal remedies help to release tension in the jaw, reducing symptoms of TMJ disorders such as jaw locking, clicking, or grinding. Examples of helpful herbs include willow bark, which reduces pain and inflammation, and passionflower, which calms nervous tension and promotes relaxation. For individuals struggling with chronic jaw pain, herbal tinctures can significantly improve their quality of life by allowing for comfortable eating, speaking, and even sleeping.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to jaw lock that are treated with herbal tinctures are various conditions characterized by muscle spasms, tension, and pain. These include headaches such as migraines and tension headaches, temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder, and fibromyalgia. Herbal tinctures containing ingredients like skullcap, passionflower, and lavender can help relax muscles, reduce inflammation, and alleviate discomfort associated with these conditions. Additionally, tinctures made from valerian root, chamomile, and ginger may also provide relief from insomnia, anxiety, and digestive issues that can contribute to jaw lock.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with jaw lock is a blend of Passionflower, Valerian Root, and Kava. Passionflower helps to calm and relax the nervous system, reducing tension and anxiety that can contribute to jaw lock. Valerian Root promotes deep relaxation and eases muscle tension, while Kava reduces inflammation and pain. This combination can be taken sublingually or added to water to help alleviate jaw lock symptoms and promote overall relaxation and well-being.
